programmes.show_description: Seth Meyers with guests. (US TX: 4 Feb 2026). (Ep1766)
On the Road to Converge: Singapore is a city built on reinvention and today, that transformation is entering a new phase.
Inside the NFL's Global Expansion Strategy: In 2025, the NFL staged a record seven regular-season games overseas as part of its bold international expansion, taking American football to Europe.
Inside Shake Shack: Julia Boorstin interviews Danny Meyer, the founder of Shake Shack - one of the fastest growing restaurant chains in America, exploring his leadership playbook.
The DNA of every successful company comes from its founders. Dig into the stories of the people behind top brands, including bubble tea franchise Gong Cha and fintech company Airwallex. (Ep1)
Just a Dollar and a Scheme / Lousy Landlord: An Iowa man rigs a $16.5 million lottery jackpot, triggering the biggest lotto scam in American history.

CNBC's Becky Quick steps out from behind the anchor desk to share her family's private battle with her daughter's rare disease.
Schneider Electric's Olivier Blum: Thrust unexpectedly into the top job, Olivier Blum reveals how resilience, discipline and constant learning shaped his rise to CEO of Schneider Electric.
Why Big Opportunities Fall Short: This edition explores why Greenland's vast mineral wealth hasn't - and likely won't - translate into prosperity for the territory.
Yungblud: Grammy-winning rock star Dom Harrison, better known as Yungblud, shows Tania Bryer the London spot that fuelled his ambition and discusses how he's using his fame to launch a fashion brand.
Novartis' Vas Narasimhan: Guided by immigrant roots, public health purpose and a belief in "staying in the ring," Novartis CEO Vas Narasimhan reflects on the unlikely decisions he made.
Capital on the Move: CNBC explains why billions of dollars are pouring into Europe's defense tech sector, plus why Asia's leading the way on stablecoin adoption. (Ep3)
Inside Popmart: CNBC takes viewers behind the scenes of China's booming IP powerhouse as it looks beyond its home market for its next wave of growth.
